title: tetrapogon pleiostachyus
intro: Tetrapogon pleiostachyus, also known as the Indian staff grass or elephant grass, is a type of perennial grass native to India and Southeast Asia. The plant is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Its health benefits are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and aid in digestion. The plant is commonly prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions for its medicinal and health benefits, although its use should be consulted with a healthcare professional.
